chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 771 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.3444/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on CMA No.3445/2025 (Ex./A) 4. For orders on CMA No.3446/2025 (S/A) 5. For hearing of main case 17.06.2025 Mr. Nazir Ahmed Junejo, Advocate for petitioner..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2 to 5. Through this petition, the petitioner has challenged the warrant of arrest issued by Banking Court-I, Sukkur Division. Learned Counsel by referring the case diary dated 07.05.2025 (available at Page-53) submits that the non-bailable warrant for 25.06.2025 has been issued against the petitioner by Banking Court-I, Sukkur Division. He further submits that the petitioner is willing to approach the Banking Court concerned in execution proceedings for installment of the decretal amount and to proceed with the matter in accordance with law. However, the warrant of arrest, issued by the Banking Court concerned, comes in the way and he has apprehension that the petitioner might be arrested. Syed Naveed Ahmed Shah, learned Deputy Attorney General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and submits that he has no objection if the warrant of arrest is suspended for a limited period, enabling the petitioner to approach the Banking Court concerned, however, subject to furnishing some surety. In view of the above, warrant of arrest issued by Banking Court-I, Sukkur against the petitioner for 25.06.2025 is hereby suspended for a C. P. No. D – 771 of 2025 Page 2 of 2 period of four days, enabling the petitioner to approach the Banking Court concerned, for redressal of his grievance, subject to furnishing a P.R. bond in the sum of Rs.5,000/- (Rupees five thousand) to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

No. D – 771 of 2025 Page 2 of 2 period of four days, enabling the petitioner to approach the Banking Court concerned, for redressal of his grievance, subject to furnishing a P.R. bond in the sum of Rs.5,000/- (Rupees five thousand) to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
